commit.message:
docs: Phase 0 baseline — Neon production numbers (follow-up to #151) (#152)

* docs: Phase 0 extraction ingestion baseline report and golden set

Add Neon SQL and GCP log query templates for stage_ms aggregation,
quality gates tied to ingest telemetry, ranked hypotheses, and a
frozen golden URL list (JSON) for future A/B extraction work.

Co-authored-by: Adam Boon <adamboon1984-arch@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs(phase0): Neon production aggregates for ingestion baseline

Fill §1.4, §2.1, §5, and §6.1–§6.5 with 90-day Neon MCP results (N=10 for
total_wall_ms cohort), document timingTelemetry coverage gap (59 vs 10),
fix §1.2 SQL to avoid invalid FILTER on ordered-set aggregates, and
record ingest_run_issues repair/retry counts. GCP log sampling skipped
where gcloud auth was unavailable.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(ops): GCP log export + script for Phase 0 timing N

Add gcloud auth runbook, FILTER for sophia + ingest-worker, and
aggregate-phase0-ingest-timing-gcp-export.ts to parse
gcloud logging read --format=json exports (widen sample vs Neon
report_envelope). Wire pnpm ops:phase0-timing-from-gcp-export.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(phase0): add multi-stage fine-tune strategy and Neon extraction shares

Integrate follow-up analysis: ~25% mean / ~38% p90 extraction share of
total_wall_ms, validation on a separate judge model, portfolio E2E
targets, new §7 handover narrative, checklist updates, and §5 spike framing.

Co-authored-by: Adam Boon <adamboon1984-arch@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: publish curated docs only; gitignore ops data

Stop tracking the large internal docs tree; keep eight public markdown files
(docs hub, SOPHIA core four, Stoa readme, Restormel stub, ingestion credits).
Add .gitignore rules for the rest of docs/ and for local batch/relabel data.

Make README and CI align: docs-lint skips missing Restormel delivery files,
update_repo_docs is a no-op, linear-sync push paths no longer reference
removed files. Trim repomix KB include list to the public docs set.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(ingest): coerce lastOutputAt for neonBumpRunActivity

lastOutputAt is optional before the first log line; fall back to createdAt
so svelte-check accepts the Neon activity bump calls.

Made-with: Cursor

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Adam Boon <adamboon1984-arch@users.noreply.github.com>

Hide Battle nav when no encounters; preserve nav bar width (#266)

* Hide Battle nav until encounters unlock; keep bar width

Use getVisibleEncounters (same as battle selector) to decide visibility.
When hidden, render an invisible layout placeholder so flex spacing matches
four nav items in portrait and landscape.

Co-authored-by: Pedro Lima e Silva <PedroLimaSilva@users.noreply.github.com>

* Nav: drop Battle placeholder; rely on space-between only

Remove the invisible slot when battles are locked. Portrait nav already
uses justify-content: space-between for the remaining items.

Co-authored-by: Pedro Lima e Silva <PedroLimaSilva@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update tests and minheight

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Pedro Lima e Silva <PedroLimaSilva@users.noreply.github.com>

- Animate displayed EXP and level bar with motion values
- Show motion LEVEL UP overlay when level increases after battle
- Export getTotalExpThresholdForLevel for shared level math

Co-authored-by: Pedro Lima e Silva <PedroLimaSilva@users.noreply.github.com>

fix + improve: UI/UX — bugs, accessibility, responsive, navbar, card hover (#1) * fix: resolve UI/UX bugs across layout, accessibility, and responsive design - orbit-dot: add transform translate(-50%,-50%) to center it on ring - secondary button: raise border opacity from 0.05 to 0.25 (was near-invisible) - footer copy: replace hardcoded #334155 with var(--text-muted) token - focus-visible: add global :focus-visible outline for keyboard navigation - mobile spacing: reduce manifesto (200→100px), tech (160→80px), footer (120→80px) padding - mobile buttons: full-width (max-width 320px) when stacked in column layout - footer links: add flex-wrap to prevent overflow on small screens - buttons: add type="button" to prevent accidental form submission - manifesto: change

to

for correct semantics - images: add sizes prop to all next/image fill usages (prevents over-fetching) - VisionCard: add meaningful alt text prop; fix card 1 using wrong image src - VisionCard: expose alt as explicit prop with descriptive defaults per card Co-authored-by: adrien-debug * improve: navbar, tablet grid, mobile spacing, card hover, consistency fixes Co-authored-by: adrien-debug --------- Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent Co-authored-by: adrien-debug

[agento] feat(upstream-port): add notifier-discord plugin from upstream (#435)

* [agento] feat(upstream-port): add notifier-discord plugin from upstream

* [agento] fix(notifier-discord): address CodeRabbit review comments

- Throw on non-Discord webhookUrl instead of console.warn (prevents silent
  data egress to wrong endpoints)
- Add truncate() helper; cap title (256), field values (1024), post content
  (2000) to Discord API limits (prevents 400 errors on long sessionIds/URLs)
- Add vi.useRealTimers() to afterEach to prevent timer leakage between tests
- Add tests: non-Discord URL throws, 429 Retry-After retry, title/content truncation
- Add secret-handling warning to README setup example

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* [agento] fix(notifier-discord): address second CodeRabbit review comments

- Enforce total embed 6000-char limit via capEmbedTotalChars()
- Map all CI_STATUS values to distinct emoji (pending/none/failing)
- Thread timeoutMs config through postWithRetry()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: register discord notifier plugin in registry and CLI dependencies

* [agento] fix(notifier-discord): address third CodeRabbit review - config validation and description truncation

---------

repository.description: Agentic orchestrator for parallel coding agents — plans tasks, spawns agents, and autonomously handles CI    fixes, merge conflicts, and code reviews.

commit.message:
Fix strict clippy and rustdoc evaluation failures (#1)

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: rishigandhe <rishigandhe@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(auth): production login — session user id + redirect callback (#186)

* fix(auth): expose user id in session and correct NextAuth redirects

- Add jwt/session callbacks so session.user.id matches Prisma user id for getActiveUser
- Fix redirect callback to allow path-only callbackUrl (default NextAuth behavior)
- Set anon_id cookie secure only in production for local HTTP compatibility
- Extend next-auth Session type for optional user id

* fix(auth): align NEXTAUTH_URL with Vercel preview and deployment URL

- Resolve canonical origin from VERCEL_URL on preview/development
- Fallback to VERCEL_URL when VERCEL=1 and NEXTAUTH_URL is unset
- Load ensureNextAuthUrl before authOptions so getServerSession sees same URL
- Add unit tests for resolveNextAuthUrl

* fix(auth): run ensureNextAuthUrl on Node server startup via instrumentation

getServerSession() can load authOptions without executing the auth route module,
so NEXTAUTH_URL was not always normalized before JWT decode. Import
ensureNextAuthUrl in instrumentation register() so every Node worker sets it.

* fix(db): begrens pg-pool op Vercel en vang DB-timeouts in credentials-login af

Gebruik een expliciete pool-config (max 5 op Vercel, configureerbaar via PG_POOL_MAX)
voor PrismaPg om connection storms te verminderen. Credentials authorize vangt
Prisma-fouten (zoals ETIMEDOUT), logt naar Sentry en geeft null i.p.v. een
ruwe error-pagina.

* fix: CodeRabbit — Sentry bij redirect/URL-parse, anon_id secure, http(s) origin

- nextauth redirect: log invalid URL naar Sentry (geen stille catch)
- link/complete: zelfde secure-cookie gedrag als middleware (alleen productie)
- nextAuthUrl: alleen echte http/https-URL’s; Sentry bij parse-fouten; test

---------

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Co-authored-by: juliamaycurran123 <juliamaycurran123@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: add .gitignore for node_modules, .next, and out

Co-authored-by: juliamaycurran123 <juliamaycurran123@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: skip shell hook confirmation prompt in Cloud Agent environments

The beforeShellExecution hook prompts for confirmation on npm install
and git push commands. In automated Cloud Agent setup flows there is
no human to approve the prompt, which causes the environment snapshot
to fail. This adds an early exit when running non-interactively.

Co-authored-by: juliamaycurran123 <juliamaycurran123@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(backfill): rescue orphaned staging data before dropping on resume (#308)

When a backfill invocation crashes after flushing data to the BQ
staging table but before merging to live, the next invocation's
prepare-staging step drops that data. This adds a rescue merge
before the drop: if the staging table has rows from a previous
crash, they get merged into the live table first.

Made-with: Cursor

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
chore(deps): bump react-dom and @types/react-dom (#37)

* chore(deps): bump react-dom and @types/react-dom

Bumps [react-dom](https://github.com/facebook/react/tree/HEAD/packages/react-dom) and [@types/react-dom](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/tree/HEAD/types/react-dom). These dependencies needed to be updated together.

Updates `react-dom` from 18.3.1 to 19.2.5
- [Release notes](https://github.com/facebook/react/releases)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/facebook/react/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md)
- [Commits](https://github.com/facebook/react/commits/v19.2.5/packages/react-dom)

Updates `@types/react-dom` from 18.3.7 to 19.2.3
- [Release notes](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/releases)
- [Commits](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/commits/HEAD/types/react-dom)

---
updated-dependencies:
- dependency-name: react-dom
  dependency-version: 19.2.5
  dependency-type: direct:production
  update-type: version-update:semver-major
- dependency-name: "@types/react-dom"
  dependency-version: 19.2.3
  dependency-type: direct:development
  update-type: version-update:semver-major
...

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>

* chore(deps): upgrade react to v19 to match react-dom

- Updated react from ^18.2.0 to ^19.2.5 to match react-dom upgrade
- Updated @types/react from ^18 to ^19 to match @types/react-dom
- Resolved peer dependency conflict causing build failures
- Verified successful build with npm run build

Co-authored-by: Amanda Nelson <jobs@amanda-nelson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>
Co-authored-by: dependabot[bot] <49699333+dependabot[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Amanda Nelson <jobs@amanda-nelson.com>

fix: improve game tile labels on /games page (#154)

- Remove meaningless player-count badge ("1" on every tile)
- Prefix difficulty badge with "AI Difficulty:" for context
- Replace "Coming Soon" with "No AI Yet" (warning badge) for the five
  playable games that don't yet have a trained AI integrated
- Pong retains "Coming Soon" as the game itself is not yet playable
- Remove "1 Player" from all tags arrays

DB update for the games table is documented in features/per-game-bugs/spec.md.

Durable ingest jobs: per-job worker defaults (extraction, store, remediation) (#150)

* feat(ingest-jobs): per-job worker defaults for durable batch UI

- Add ingestion_jobs.worker_defaults jsonb (migration 0014) and Drizzle column.
- Sanitize API payload; merge into each child ingest batch_overrides when the
  job ticks (extraction/store concurrency, remediation caps, watchdog JSON, etc.).
- Durable jobs page: collapsible worker defaults + localStorage persistence.
- Restore batch_overrides env mapping for passage/claim concurrency and watchdog
  tuning in ingestRuns (used by jobs + single-run paths).

spec: per-game bugs (#96-99), cross-game conventions, ADR (#152)

* spec: per-game bug fixes (issues #96-#99)

Game-specific bugs not covered by UX standardization:
- Connect4: column click zone + hover preview
- Dots & Boxes: box fill icons, button alignment
- Chess: board orientation, piece images, AN notation, FEN storage, captures, scrolling
- Checkers: forced capture visual, turn indicator, AI timing delay
Depends on ux-game-standardization being merged first.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* spec: add games page label bugs to per-game-bugs

Documents the three display issues on /games: missing difficulty context,
useless player count badge, and "Coming Soon" applied to playable games.
Notes that frontend changes are already implemented; DB update SQL included.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* spec: fix DB SQL to use array_remove/array_append (tags is text[])

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* spec: document PR checkout, PGN regen, global event pacing, optimistic UX

Record cross-game conventions in per-game-bugs: FEN plus SAN move_list with
PGN regenerated on demand; single GAME_SERVER_MIN_EVENT_INTERVAL_MS for all
SSE games; optimistic animations vs authoritative rejection handling;
explicit not-doing for premove and deferred checkers path UX. CONTRIBUTING
adds gh pr checkout guidance so review edits land on the PR branch.

* docs: PR body maintenance, ADR policy, per-game-bugs ADR

CONTRIBUTING: require PR title/body updates during review; document ADR
workflow and link ADRs from PR descriptions. CLAUDE.md: align feature
steps with CONTRIBUTING. Add features/per-game-bugs/adr.md for cross-game
move storage, global SSE pacing, and optimistic UI; link from spec.

* docs: Cursor auth failures - ask user for gh/gcloud on Windows

Add CONTRIBUTING §1e: on GitHub/GCP permission errors, direct the user to
complete gh auth login and gcloud auth flows on their machine (Credential
Manager), never tokens in chat; note gh pr edit vs REST workaround and
cloud-only agent caveat. CLAUDE.md points to §1e.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

Kitchen-first UI: bottom nav, PRINT FAB, collapsed More menu (#2)

* feat: complete Cheftag Solutions app

- Next.js 15 (App Router) + React 19 + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui v4
- Dashboard with 6 KPI cards, 3 pie charts, bar chart, recent activity
- Labels page with grid view, filters, bulk actions, quick print
- Products/Employees/Groups pages with TanStack Table v8
- Devices page with card layout and test print
- Reports page with full table + CSV/PDF export
- Preservation Modes and Settings management pages
- Printing page with queue and device selection
- Zustand store with localStorage persistence
- i18n translation system (English, ready for pt-BR)
- Purple/Gold color scheme, dark mode support
- Mobile bottom navigation, responsive design
- Keyboard shortcuts (N = new label, / = focus search)

spec: test audit and /bug regression tool (#153)

* spec: test audit and /bug regression tool

Audits coverage gaps (4 games missing E2E, leaderboard, chess FEN),
identifies structural issues (two parallel API test suites, JS/TS mismatch,
seed password inconsistency), adds leaderboard + chess FEN tests, adds
E2E for all 5 games, and introduces a /bug slash command for creating
failing regression tests before fixes are written.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: Plan as first-class object — Explore/Propose/Execute separation (#42)

* refactor: plan as first-class object with explore/propose/execute separation

Core architecture changes:
- New plan.py: Plan data model with id, status, persistence in .meta/plans/
- chat() no longer intercepts write tools; model uses observation + submit_plan
- New execute_plan(): separate LLM call to faithfully implement approved plans
- System verifies model's change_type classification (policy.py)
- Incremental plans auto-execute; structural plans require user approval
- CLI and gateway updated for new plan-based approval flow
- Prompts rewritten: exploration/planning vs execution separation
- Legacy list[dict] backward compatibility preserved

Co-authored-by: forjiuzhou <forjiuzhou@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: update tests for new plan architecture, add test_plan.py

- Rewrite test_integration.py tests for submit_plan + execute_plan flow
- Rewrite test_audit.py write interception tests for plan-based architecture
- Update test_prompt_engine.py for new prompt structure
- Add test_plan.py: 26 tests covering Plan model, PlanStore, staleness,
  legacy migration, and classify_change_type
- Update CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md for new architecture

All 583 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: forjiuzhou <forjiuzhou@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: forjiuzhou <forjiuzhou@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve broken ticket submission forms (#18)

Two issues fixed:

1. submit-ticket.mdx - Existing Ticket Lookup form:
   - Removed inline POST form with hardcoded stale CSRF authenticity_token
   - Rails generates per-session CSRF tokens, so a static token baked into
     the MDX page will never validate, causing submissions to silently fail
   - Replaced with a direct link to the Syncro check_ins portal where the
     form renders natively with a valid session-bound token

2. submit-ticket.mdx - New Service Ticket form:
   - Removed noValidate attribute so browser enforces required email field
   - Added target=_blank so the form submits to Syncro in a new tab,
     preventing Mintlify SPA routing from intercepting the submission

3. client-login.mdx - Client Portal Login form:
   - Updated form action from /my_profile/do_login to /portal/do_login
     (the old URL 302-redirects, which drops POST body in browsers)
   - Added target=_blank for reliable cross-origin form submission

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>

commit.message:
fix(neon): use alias ir in idle watchdog candidate SQL (#149)

FROM ingest_runs ir cannot reference unqualified ingest_runs columns;
Drizzle's idleStallWhereSql expanded to "ingest_runs"."last_output_at"
which fails in PostgreSQL and broke durable jobs UI refresh.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: Adam Boon <adamboon1984-arch@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve admin setup and login redirect loop (#42)

Three bugs combined to prevent a user from staying logged in after
creating the admin account or signing in:

1. SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE defaulted to True in config.py.
   Browsers silently discard Secure cookies over plain HTTP, so the
   session cookie was never stored after login/setup, making every
   subsequent API call return 401.  Changed default to False to match
   the docker-compose.yml default and support the majority of
   self-hosted (LAN/HTTP) deployments out of the box.  Operators
   behind HTTPS must opt in with SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=true.

2. The global Axios 401 interceptor only exempted /login.
   Any 401 received while the browser was on /setup, /forgot-password,
   or /reset-password triggered a hard redirect to /login.  The initial
   unauthenticated GET /auth/me probe (expected to 401) would therefore
   navigate away from the setup wizard.  Added those public paths to
   the exemption list.

3. Race condition between the initial /auth/me probe and a successful
   login.  If the probe's 401 arrived after navigate('/') was called,
   the interceptor saw a non-login path and bounced the user back.
   Fixed by holding an AbortController ref for the probe in
   AuthProvider and aborting it inside login() before the credentials
   POST resolves.  Cancelled requests are also now silently ignored by
   the interceptor (axios.isCancel check).

Updated test_config.py to assert the new False default.

Co-authored-by: Cursor Agent <cursoragent@cursor.com>
Co-authored-by: titatom <titatom@users.noreply.github.com>
